messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
Which Languages are spoken by more than 3.5% of the world population

content.message:
To determine which languages are spoken by more than 3.5% of the world population, I need to:

1. Identify relevant properties related to languages and population.
2. Find entities that represent languages and their associated population data.
3. Filter languages where the percentage of the world population speaking them exceeds 3.5%.

### Step 1: Search for relevant properties related to languages and population
I will search for properties related to "language" and "population" in Wikidata to understand which properties might capture language and population data.

I'll start by searching for properties related to "language".

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. language of work or name (wd:P407 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language"), also known as "audio language", "available in", "broadcasting language", "language", "language of name", etc.:
  - language associated with this creative work (such as books, ... (P103) and "languages spoken, written or signed" (P1412))
2. languages spoken, written or signed (wd:P1412 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language"), also known as "language", "language of expression", "language read", "language signed", "languages of expression", etc.:
  - language(s) that a person or a people speaks, writes or signs, including the native language(s)
3. programmed in (wd:P277 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language"), also known as "coded in", "language", "programming language", "written in":
  - the programming language(s) in which the software is developed
4. language used (wd:P2936 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "working language"), also known as "languages used", "medium of instruction", "used language", "uses language", "working language", etc.:
  - language widely used (spoken or written) in this place or at this event or organisation
5. writing language (wd:P6886 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "languages written"), also known as "language for writing", "language of works", "language of writings", "languages for writing", "languages of works", etc.:
  - language in which the writer has written their work
6. language style (wd:P6191 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language use"), also known as "archaic", "childish", "context", "dated", "form register", etc.:
  - to denote of the way a sense of a word is used, e.g., slang, vulgarism, babytalk, colloquialism, etc.
7. Ethnologue language status (wd:P3823 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language status"), also known as "EGIDS", "language status":
  - language status identifier by Ethnologue.com using EGIDS scale
8. language regulatory body (wd:P1018 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language authority"), also known as "language authority", "regulatory body":
  - regulatory body of a language
9. location (wd:P276 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locale"), also known as "based in", "city", "exact location", "from", "held by", etc.:
  - location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to ... entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object
10. IETF language tag (wd:P305 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "language code"), also known as "BCP 47 language code", "ISO language code IETF implementation", "language code", "language tag IETF":
  - identifier for language or languoid per the Internet ... Format: 2 or 3 letters, followed by "-" if subtags present

content.message:
I need to find properties related to population percentages for languages. From the results, the property "languages spoken, written or signed" (P1412) seems the most relevant. Next, I'll search for properties related to population or percentage.

I will now search for properties related to population or percentage.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. tabular population (wd:P4179 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "population"), also known as "population":
  - same as population (P1082); used when there is data for many different points in time
2. population (wd:P1082 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "human population", "inhabitants":
  - number of people inhabiting the place; number of people of subject
3. intended public (wd:P2360 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "demographic"), also known as "aimed at", "audience", "audience of", "created for", "demographic", etc.:
  - intended audience or user of this work, product, object, or event
4. male population (wd:P1540 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "male inhabitants", "number of males":
  - number of male people inhabiting the place; number of male people of subject
5. urban population (wd:P6343 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - number of people living within the territorial entity who live in its urban parts
6. rural population (wd:P6344 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - number of people living within the territorial entity who live in its rural parts
7. category populated by (wd:P4329 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "populated by"), also known as "category filled by", "category has template", "cat populated by", "component adding into the category", "populated by", etc.:
  - software component (template, module, extension, MediaWiki core) populating the category
8. occupant (wd:P466 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "inhabitant"), also known as "home team", "houses", "inhabitant", "lessee", "location of", etc.:
  - person or organization occupying property
9. ethnic group (wd:P172 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "people"), also known as "(cultural) nationality", "culture", "ethnicity", "ethnic or cultural origin", "people", etc.:
  - subject's ethnicity (consensus is that a VERY high standard ... on by scholars, or 3) is fictional and portrayed as such)
10. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "nation"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen

content.message:
I need to find a property related to percentage of population for languages. The property "population" (P1082) is relevant, but I need to check if there's a specific property related to the percentage of the world population speaking a language.

I'll search for properties related to "percentage" or "share".

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. proportion (wd:P1107 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "percent"), also known as "%", "part", "percent", "ratio", "share":
  - to be used as a qualifier, value must be between 0 and 1
2. relative position within image (wd:P2677 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "pct:"), also known as "IIIF syntax relative position", "image map", "location in the image", "location within image", "pct:", etc.:
  - position of a motif within a larger image, defined by ... the crop region (as a percentage of the whole image) values
3. aspect ratio (W:H) (wd:P2061 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "ratio"), also known as "aspect ratio", "image aspect ratio", "ratio":
  - image width to height ratio of flags, films, photographs, and other images
4. applies to part (wd:P518 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "per"), also known as "applied to", "applies to aspect", "applies to form", "applies to phase", "apply to", etc.:
  - part, aspect, or form of the item to which the claim applies
5. statement supported by (wd:P3680 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "per"), also known as "according to", "claimed by", "per", "recognized by", "stated by", etc.:
  - entity that supports a given statement
6. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "division"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
7. price (wd:P2284 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "value"), also known as "cost", "exchange rate", "list price", "MSRP", "published price", etc.:
  - published price listed or paid for a product (use with unit of currency)
8. prize money (wd:P2121 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "value"), also known as "money", "purse", "value":
  - amount in a specific currency
9. item of property constraint (wd:P2305 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "value"), also known as "allowed entity types", "allowed unit", "claim value", "item", "item of constraint", etc.:
  - qualifier to define a property constraint in combination with "property constraint" (P2302)
10. main subject (wd:P921 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of"), also known as "about", "artistic theme", "/common/topic/subject", "content deals with", "content describes", etc.:
  - primary topic of a work or act of communication

content.message:
I now have enough information to proceed. The relevant properties are:

- "languages spoken, written or signed" (P1412) – to identify languages.
- "proportion" (P1107) – to identify the percentage of the population speaking a language.

I will now search for languages that are associated with a proportion (percentage) greater than 3.5% of the world population. I'll first search for languages with proportion data.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. different from (wd:P1889 as wdt/p, matched via "not"), also known as "confused with", "different than", "different to", "differs from", "diff from", etc.:
  - item that is different from another item, with which it may be confused
2. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "type"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
3. child (wd:P40 as wdt/p, matched via "progeny"), also known as "children", "daughter", "daughters", "descendants", "father of", etc.:
  - subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
4. spouse (wd:P26 as wdt/p, matched via "partner"), also known as "common-law husband", "common-law spouse", "common-law wife", "husband", "husbands", etc.:
  - the subject has the object as their spouse (husband, wife, ... Use "unmarried partner" (P451) for non-married companions
5. NDL Authority ID (wd:P349 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "ndl"), also known as "National Diet Library Auth ID", "National Diet Library ID", "National Diet Library of Japan authority control ID", "National Diet Library of Japan ID", "ndl", etc.:
  - identifier for authority control per the National Diet Library of Japan
6. notable work (wd:P800 as wdt/p, matched via "significance"), also known as "artwork", "author of", "bibliography", "books by", "creator of", etc.:
  - notable scientific, artistic or literary work, or other work of significance among subject's works
7. field of work (wd:P101 as wdt/p, matched via "area"), also known as "academic area", "academic discipline", "academic subject", "activity", "activity domain", etc.:
  - specialization of a person or organization; see P106 for the occupation
8. participant in (wd:P1344 as wdt/p, matched via "takes part"), also known as "attended", "competed in", "competes in", "participant of", "participant of event", etc.:
  - event in which a person, organization or creative work was/is a participant; inverse of P710 or P1923
9. described by source (wd:P1343 as wdt/p, matched via "mentioned in"), also known as "dbs", "def in", "defined in", "desc by biography", "desc by encyclopedia", etc.:
  - work where this item is described, in statistical contexts, a methodological note describing the data
10. permanent duplicated item (wd:P2959 as wdt/p, matched via "identical to"), also known as "duplicate", "duplicate item", "duplicate of", "duplicate of item", "identical to", etc.:
  - this item duplicates another item and the two can't be ... gomwiki, nanwiki). Use P31=Q17362920 for other wikis.
